title: Healey calls on aviation company operating at Hanscom to cut ties with ICE
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Governor Healey urged Signature Aviation to end collaboration with ICE flights at Hanscom Field, citing civil rights concerns.
Healey criticized ICE's mass deportation campaign and claimed private aviation aids in ICE's deportation operations at Hanscom.
Signature Aviation, the sole operator for ICE at Hanscom, did not respond to requests for comment about Healey's demands.

title: Delivery person falls through a shaft outside Boston’s Omni Parker House
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A delivery worker fell 20 feet and was injured after a metal grate collapsed outside Boston's Omni Parker House.
The Boston Inspectional Services is issuing a building code violation due to failed vent grate supporting brackets.
The hotel was ordered to hire a structural engineer to inspect and report on all vent grates for necessary repairs.

title: Quincy gas station looking for violist to ‘serenade’ customers
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Quincy Gas owner Harvey Kertzman seeks a violist to perform for customers at his gas station on Quincy Avenue.
Kertzman has posted signs for over a year and recently found a retired violist to start in warm weather.
Signs remain up at Quincy Gas for potential violists, while Kertzman offers unique station decorations like inspirational quotes.

title: Report: Patriots agree to sign edge rusher Dre’Mont Jones
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Patriots agreed to sign edge rusher Dre’Mont Jones, as reported by NFL Media's Ian Rapoport and Tom Pellissero.
Jones's deal with the Patriots is valued up to $39.5 million over three years, per reports.
Jones, formerly with the Ravens and Titans, recorded seven sacks and 24 QB hits last season.

title: How a Boston Marathon runner finds purpose in mentoring and miles
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Victoria Walker, 31, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Minds Matter Boston, combining her passion for mentoring and running.
Walker mentors high-achieving, low-income students through Minds Matter Boston, helping them with college applications and building confidence.
Running provides Walker with community and purpose, motivating her to raise funds for her mentees' educational pursuits during the marathon.

title: Meet the New England Aquarium’s viral seal and his rubber duckie pal
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Reggae, a harbor seal at the New England Aquarium, went viral while playing with a rubber duckie toy.
The video of Reggae has nearly 500,000 views and 100,000 engagements, highlighting harbor seals' intelligence.
The aquarium's training staff uses cognitive enrichment activities for seals, simulating behaviors they'd use in the wild.

title: What we know about Bravo’s ‘The Real Housewives of Rhode Island’
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: 'The Real Housewives of Rhode Island' premieres April 2, featuring a cast of Rhode Island notables including a pizzeria co-owner and a former TV anchor.
Bravo night at Fenway Park with the RHORI cast takes place April 17 during the Red Sox vs. Detroit Tigers game.
The series delves into Rhode Island's social scene, exploring friendships and feuds within a tight-knit community with deep Italian roots.

title: 24 Boston businesses approved for new or upgraded liquor licenses
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Boston approved 24 new or upgraded liquor licenses for restaurants, aiding businesses in diverse neighborhoods like East Boston and Roslindale.
Legislation from 2024 and prior enabled these approvals, with zip code-restricted licenses helping to distribute opportunities citywide.
Six businesses upgraded their licenses from beer-and-wine to all-alcohol, expanding their beverage offerings starting in 2026.

title: Mass. man nabbed after allegedly driving over 100 mph in N.H.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 21-year-old Attleboro man was arrested for allegedly driving 104 mph in a 55 mph zone on Route 202 in Rindge, NH.
The incident occurred shortly after 11 p.m. near Sears Drive, according to Rindge Police Chief Rachel Malynowski.
The driver faces charges of reckless operation, potential fines, and a 90-day license suspension; arraignment is set for April 5.

title: AirTag leads volunteers to hidden cat and six kittens in Waltham
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Volunteers from The Cat Connection used an AirTag to locate and rescue a stray cat and six newborn kittens.
Emerald, the mother cat, led rescuers to her kittens at a self-storage facility in Waltham amid a blizzard.
The Cat Connection plans to make Emerald and her kittens available for adoption in about two months.
